pages.text: Deep in the Moonberry Mountains lived a small dragon named Ember. While all his dragon friends soared above the clouds, Ember stayed on the ground.
Ember tried and tried to fly, flapping his wings as hard as he could. But every time, he just tumbled gently into a pile of soft purple flowers.
One day, a little bunny named Bea saw Ember looking sad. 'I can't hop as far as other bunnies,' said Bea. 'But I make the best carrot soup!'
Ember smiled. He realized that he could breathe the warmest, coziest fire — perfect for keeping all his friends warm on cold nights.
That night, Ember sat with all his friends under the stars, keeping them warm and cozy. He wasn't so different after all — he was just perfectly himself. Goodnight, little dragon.
On a quiet Tuesday evening, a tiny fairy named Luna appeared on Olivia's windowsill, glowing like a little pearl.
Luna held a small silver wand. 'I carry moonbeams,' she whispered, 'one for every sleeping child who needs a sweet dream.'
She sprinkled a moonbeam over Olivia's pillow — it turned into a soft, glowing cloud that smelled like vanilla and warm blankets.
'Dream of anywhere you want to go,' said Luna, 'because in dreams, everything is possible.' Olivia felt her eyes grow heavy and warm.
Luna flew back to the moon, leaving a trail of soft silver light. Olivia closed her eyes and drifted away to the sweetest adventure. Goodnight, little one.
Far above the clouds, in the velvet night sky, lived millions of stars — and every night they had one very important job.
The stars had to shine brightly until every child on Earth was safely asleep. It was the most important job in the whole universe.
Lucas, a curious little star, peeked down through a telescope cloud and saw a little boy still awake, reading with a flashlight.
'If you go to sleep,' Lucas twinkled in morse code, 'I'll send you the best dream — one with rocket ships and rainbow planets!'
The little boy smiled, tucked under his blanket, and closed his eyes. And Lucas the star shone his very brightest, all through the night. Sweet dreams, little astronaut.
Beneath the shimmering waves lived a curious little fish named Pearl. Every day she swam past the same coral reef, wondering what lay beyond the blue horizon.
One morning, Pearl met a wise old sea turtle named Tide. 'I know a place,' said Tide slowly, 'where the coral sings lullabies at night.'
They swam together past glowing jellyfish and sleeping starfish, through tunnels of sea glass that sparkled like underwater stars.
At last they reached the singing coral reef. The soft hum filled the water like a warm hug, and Pearl felt her fins grow still and peaceful.
Pearl curled up in a cozy shell and listened to the coral's gentle song. The whole ocean grew quiet and calm. Goodnight, little explorer.
In the Whispering Woods, where mushrooms wore tiny hats and fireflies carried lanterns, lived a little fox named Finn.
Finn loved to explore, but one evening he walked so far that he found himself in a clearing he had never seen — filled with the softest, most golden moss.
A wise old owl hooted from above. 'This is the Dreaming Meadow,' she said softly. 'All who rest here have the most wonderful dreams.'
Finn lay down on the golden moss. The fireflies dimmed their lanterns one by one, and the trees began to hum a sleepy, gentle tune.
Finn closed his bright eyes and dreamed of meadows full of butterflies and warm sunshine. The whole forest breathed slowly with him. Goodnight, little fox.
